goal: Build a configurable v0.2 tank match foundation with lobby tank selection, seeded terrain generation, ammo and reload rules, health UI, and capturable map points while preserving the v0.1 arcade tank loop.

project_context.latest_confirmed_commit_before_plan: 79e0bd5 Improve tank terrain visibility and camera aiming
current_foundation: Basic tank scene exists with LowPolyBody, TurretPivot, Muzzle, AimLine, projectile spawning, terrain sampling, slope slowdown, and simple health.
VehicleStats resource exists and is assigned through the Tank scene.
TerrainWorld can generate terrain heights and exposes get_height_at/get_normal_at for tanks.
Terrain3D is installed and used as data/collision carrier, with a v0.1 preview mesh for readable visuals.
Lobby start screen already has host/join/copy/paste/start, connection status, ping display, loading overlay, and map preview placeholder.
VehicleTest scene exists for local gameplay smoke tests.

design_principles: Prefer node-, scene-, resource-, project-setting-, and inspector-based implementation.
Use code only for runtime behavior, deterministic terrain generation, combat state, capture progress, and network authority boundaries.
Use resources for tunable gameplay data: vehicle definitions, vehicle stats, weapon stats, terrain generation settings, capture point settings, and map definitions.
Keep v0.2 playable with one local instance first, then wire lobby-selected data so multiplayer sync can use the same contracts.
Host is the future authority for selected map seed, match settings, capture progress, ammo consumption, damage, and respawn.
Avoid hardcoded vehicle/model logic in TankController; selection should swap resources/scenes through inspector-friendly definitions.

must_have: Multiple tank definitions with different model/stat/resource presets.
Tank selection UI in the lobby before Start Match.
Selected tank applied when the match starts.
Ammo count and reload speed affect firing.
Health bar UI for the local tank and readable target health feedback.
At least two capture points placed on the map, capturable by standing inside their Area3D.
Seeded terrain generation controlled from the lobby.
Map preview updates from selected seed/settings at a basic level.
Focused local tests and scene boot checks for VehicleTest and TankGame.
should_have: Three initial vehicle definitions: Basic Tank, Light Tank, Heavy Tank.
At least one vehicle variant should visibly differ in speed, HP, reload, ammo, and slope handling.
Capture point UI should show neutral, capturing, contested, and owned states.
Lobby should show selected tank stats and selected seed before match start.
Host-only map seed editing when multiplayer peer is active.
could_have: Simple player name labels above tanks.
Per-vehicle preview mesh in the lobby map preview area.
Copy match settings payload for debugging.
explicit_non_goals: No directional hit zones or subsystem damage.
No drones, spider tanks, or anti-drone countermeasures.
No full teams, scoreboard polish, respawn tickets, or match end cinematics.
No final Terrain3D painting workflow; v0.2 can still use generated preview mesh visuals if Terrain3D texture assets are not configured.
No destructive terrain craters unless they fall out naturally from seeded generation work without destabilizing v0.2.

vehicle_definition.purpose: Designer-facing resource selected in the lobby and applied to spawned tanks.

fields.vehicle_id: StringName, stable network/save id such as basic_tank
fields.visual_scene: PackedScene, optional visual/model override if Tank scene remains shared
fields.preview_color: Color for UI swatches and map preview

acceptance: A tank can be selected by stable vehicle_id instead of by file path.
A designer can add a new vehicle definition without editing TankGame code.
Lobby can enumerate an exported array or folder-loaded list of VehicleDefinition resources.

vehicle_stats_extensions.note: Keep combat-specific weapon fields in WeaponStats instead of overloading VehicleStats further.

fields.line_of_fire_required: bool, default false for arcade shell tests

v02_policy: Use single-shot reload first: one shell can be fired when reload timer is complete.
Ammo capacity is total available shells for the match.
Reload starts immediately after a successful shot if ammo remains.
No magazine/clip system in v0.2 unless the single-shot system is already stable.

determinism_contract: The same settings and seed must generate the same terrain heights on every peer.
Do not use randomize() or non-seeded RNG for generated gameplay geometry.
Lobby owns the selected seed before match start; host broadcasts selected seed/settings later in multiplayer.

feature_tracks.title: Vehicle Definitions And Lobby Tank Selection
feature_tracks.goal: Replace the single hardcoded basic tank choice with resource-driven selectable vehicle definitions.
ordered_tasks: Create VehicleDefinition resource class.
Create Basic, Light, and Heavy tank definition resources.
Move current Basic tank weapon values out of VehicleStats into WeaponStats or bridge fields with backwards compatibility.
Add an inspector-configured vehicle_definitions array to LobbyStartScreen or TankGame.
Add lobby selection UI using OptionButton or ItemList, plus compact stat preview.
Store selected vehicle_id for the local player.
On Start Match, apply selected definition to PreviewTank or spawned player tank.
Keep default Basic Tank selected if no valid selection exists.

initial_vehicle_targets.stats_intent: Baseline speed, baseline health, baseline reload, moderate slope slowdown.
Higher speed and turret turn, lower health, lower damage or lower ammo, stronger slope penalty.
Higher health and damage, slower movement/turret, longer reload, weaker slope speed but more durability.

acceptance: Lobby shows at least three selectable tank definitions.
Each selection shows HP, speed, reload, ammo, and damage.
Starting match applies selected stats to the playable tank.
No tank controller code contains per-vehicle if/else branches for the three vehicle types.

feature_tracks.title: Ammo And Reload Loop
feature_tracks.goal: Make firing depend on ammo and reload timing with clear HUD feedback.
ordered_tasks: Create WeaponStats resource class and presets.
Add exported weapon_stats to TankController or VehicleDefinition.
Track current_ammo, reload_remaining, and can_fire state in TankController.
Block firing while reload_remaining > 0 or current_ammo <= 0.
Emit ammo_changed and reload_changed signals from TankController.
Update projectile launch to read damage/speed/projectile_scene from WeaponStats.
Add simple click feedback/status when empty or reloading.
Add reload progress display to HUD.
acceptance: Firing reduces ammo by one.
Reload timer prevents immediate repeat firing.
Reload speed differs between vehicle definitions.
Ammo count and reload progress are visible in the match HUD.
Script checks pass and local firing smoke test confirms cooldown/ammo behavior.

feature_tracks.title: Health Bars And Combat Feedback
feature_tracks.goal: Expose tank health changes through signals and show readable HP bars.
ordered_tasks: Add max_health/current_health naming to TankController while preserving existing health behavior.
Emit health_changed(current, max) after setup and damage.
Create MatchHud scene with HP ProgressBar, ammo label, reload bar, selected tank label, and capture status label.
Attach MatchHud under TankGame UI CanvasLayer.
Bind local tank signals to MatchHud when match starts.
Optionally add simple floating HP bar for target dummy or enemy tank testing.
Update target dummy to expose health_changed signal or compatible API.
acceptance: Local player HP bar appears after Start Match.
HP bar changes when damage is applied.
Ammo and reload UI update without polling fragile node paths.
UI remains usable at 1280x720 and does not cover lobby controls before match start.

feature_tracks.title: Capture Points
feature_tracks.goal: Add map objectives that can be captured by standing inside Area3D zones.
ordered_tasks: Create CapturePointSettings resource.
Create CapturePoint scene with Area3D, CollisionShape3D, MeshInstance3D ring/marker, label/progress display, and script.
Detect tank bodies entering/exiting via group or exported accepted_body_group.
Track occupants by side/team placeholder: neutral/player/enemy for local v0.2.
Increase capture progress while one side occupies and no contesting side is present.
Neutralize owned point before switching owner if needed.
Emit owner_changed and progress_changed signals.
Place at least two capture points in VehicleTest and TankGame world.
Show capture point state in MatchHud.

v02_capture_rules.teams: Use placeholder sides for now: local_player, opponent, neutral.
v02_capture_rules.capture: Standing in the point fills progress over capture_seconds.
v02_capture_rules.contest: Multiple opposing sides inside the point pauses progress.
v02_capture_rules.ownership: When progress reaches 1.0, owner changes and progress stays full.
v02_capture_rules.score: Score ticker can be debug-only in v0.2.

acceptance: Driving into a neutral capture point starts progress.
Leaving the point stops progress.
Progress reaches captured state after configured seconds.
Visual marker changes color/state when neutral, capturing, contested, or owned.
Capture point settings are tunable in the inspector.

feature_tracks.title: Seeded Dynamic Terrain Generation
feature_tracks.goal: Make terrain generation configurable from lobby seed/settings and deterministic enough for multiplayer later.
ordered_tasks: Create TerrainGenerationSettings resource.
Refactor TerrainWorld hardcoded _height formula to consume settings and seed.
Use RandomNumberGenerator with explicit seed for ridge/feature placement.
Keep generated terrain readable with current preview mesh and Terrain3D collision data.
Add apply_generation_settings(settings) to TerrainWorld.
Add get_generation_fingerprint() for debug display and future network validation.
Add lobby Seed LineEdit/SpinBox and Randomize Seed button.
Add host-only editing rules when connected to multiplayer.
Send selected seed/settings from LobbyStartScreen to TankGame on Start Match.
Regenerate TerrainWorld before match begins.
Update map preview placeholder with seed text and basic terrain color/thumbnail cue.
acceptance: Entering the same seed generates the same terrain shape after Start Match.
Different seeds visibly change terrain layout.
Terrain heights, preview mesh, and Terrain3D collision are rebuilt together.
Tank slope sampling still works after regeneration.
Lobby displays selected seed and match world displays the same seed/fingerprint.

feature_tracks.title: Match Flow Integration
feature_tracks.goal: Bundle selected tank, selected seed, terrain settings, and objective setup into one match start payload.
ordered_tasks: Create MatchSetup resource or dictionary contract with vehicle_id, map_id, seed, and mode.
LobbyStartScreen emits start_match_requested(match_setup) instead of no-argument signal, with backwards-compatible adapter if needed.
TankGame validates match_setup and falls back to default values.
TankGame applies seed to TerrainWorld before showing world.
TankGame applies selected VehicleDefinition to the player tank before enabling input.
TankGame binds HUD to the player tank and capture points.
Update loading overlay steps to show validating setup, generating terrain, spawning tank, binding HUD, ready.
acceptance: Single-player/no-peer Start Match still works.
Lobby-selected tank and seed affect the match world.
Loading feedback names the current match preparation step.
No fragile hardcoded UI paths leak into gameplay scripts beyond explicit exported NodePaths.

feature_tracks.title: Network Readiness Contracts
feature_tracks.goal: Prepare v0.2 systems so multiplayer authority can be added without rewriting feature code.
ordered_tasks: Document stable ids: vehicle_id, map_id, seed, capture_point_id, peer_id.
Ensure match setup payload contains only deterministic primitive values and stable ids, not raw Resource instances.
Ensure capture point state can be replicated as owner_id/progress/occupants summary.
Ensure ammo/reload state can be host-authoritative later.
Ensure damage events can remain host-validated before applying HP changes.
Add debug labels or logs for selected ids only; do not log secrets or Steam credentials.
acceptance: Roadmap/runbook identifies what is local-only in v0.2 and what must be host-authoritative later.
No new feature relies on random local-only state after match start.
No future network payload requires serializing Godot Resource objects directly.

abc_execution_plan.note: This roadmap is prepared for ABC execution, but no Alice/Bob/Charlie delegation is started by this file alone.

suggested_slices.goal: Save v0.2 scope and operator language.
Create resource model and three initial tank definitions.
Wire selected stats, weapon stats, ammo/reload, and health signals into runtime tank behavior.
Add lobby tank selection, seed input, settings preview, and operator docs.
Make TerrainWorld deterministic and configurable from seed/settings.
Create match HUD and bind HP/ammo/reload/capture status.
Implement node-based capture point scene and place initial objectives.
Run focused Godot checks, manual MCP scene smoke tests, commit, and push.

scene_smoke_tests: Run res://scenes/vehicle_test/vehicle_test.tscn for local tank, ammo, HP, capture, and terrain seed checks.
Run res://scenes/tank_game/tank_game.tscn and start match from lobby with selected tank and seed.
MCP screenshot check: terrain visible, HUD visible, selected tank spawned, capture points visible.
MCP runtime check: same seed produces same TerrainWorld fingerprint across two starts.
MCP runtime check: firing reduces ammo and reload progress changes.
MCP runtime check: damage changes HP bar.
MCP runtime check: standing in capture point changes capture progress.
manual_gates: Lobby tank selection feels understandable and compact.
Map seed controls are visible but do not clutter the basic lobby workflow.
Capture point markers are readable from the top-down camera.
At least one vehicle variant has a clearly different driving/combat feel.

stop_rules: Stop on secrets, Steam credentials, tokens, or private IDs.
Stop if TerrainWorld determinism cannot be verified and the next slice depends on it.
Stop if adding vehicle definitions requires rewriting the Steam lobby bootstrap.
Stop if Godot script checks fail and the fix would require out-of-scope rewrites.
Stop before commit/push if branch or remote state is unclear.

go_language.go: v0.2 is Go when TankGame starts from lobby with selected tank and seed, generated terrain is visible and deterministic, ammo/reload/HP HUD works, and at least two capture points can be captured locally.
go_language.partial: Vehicle selection and combat HUD work, but capture points or seeded terrain need another slice.
go_language.no_go: Tank selection cannot be applied cleanly through resources, or terrain generation cannot remain deterministic.
go_language.deferred: Full multiplayer authority, armor deflection, directional damage zones, drones, spider tanks, polished scoring, and final Terrain3D texture painting.

features: Spawn one selected tank per peer.
Visual hit feedback by hit zone.
Track/turret subsystem damage as a later extension.
Spider tanks with low slope penalty.
